sentence_reviews.text: Compute-optimal training allocates a finite budget among parameters, tokens, steps, data quality, and hardware so expected performance is maximized.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Reviewed as synthesis across cited source pointers and, where applicable, the canonical atlas graph context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Former ambiguity resolved by narrowing the audit interpretation to synthesis support rather than requiring exact-source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: It avoids expensive undertraining or oversizing and makes scaling decisions explicit before a large run begins.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann et al. 2022 and Kaplan et al. 2020 study empirical scaling laws and compute allocation among model size, data/tokens, and training compute for language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Chinchilla directly argues many large language models were undertrained and that compute-optimal allocation changes scaling decisions.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann et al. 2022 and Kaplan et al. 2020 study empirical scaling laws and compute allocation among model size, data/tokens, and training compute for language models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Chinchilla is directly about compute-optimal parameter-token allocation.
